headline: Dow, Nasdaq Turn Lower to Wrap Wild Week
summary: Sideways trading for stocks continued heading into the final hour of the week. The Dow was down 40 points, or 0.1%. It would be rising if not for steep drops in Goldman Sachs and Caterpillar, which are two of the largest stocks in the stock-price-weighted index.

headline: What They Aren’t Telling You About GPIQ’s “Safe” Tech-Income Strategy
summary: GPIQ has paid a monthly distribution every month since late 2023, with the June 2026 check landing at $0.52 per share. That headline yield, sitting somewhere near 10%, is why the Goldman Sachs Nasdaq-100 Premium Income ETF (NASDAQ:GPIQ) pulled in $2.12 billion of new money in 2025 alone, ballooning to roughly $2.21 billion in assets ... What They Aren’t Telling You About GPIQ’s “Safe” Tech-Income Strategy

headline: Nasdaq Rises. Caterpillar and Goldman Are Dragging on the Dow.
summary: Stocks were cruising along in Friday trading, and even the Nasdaq was in on the action.  The Dow rose 100 points, or 0.2%.  The index was rising, even though declines for Caterpillar and Goldman Sachs combined were shaving off more than 400 points on their own.

headline: Goldman Sachs Sees Stronger Freight Recovery Driving Upside for TFI International (TFII)

headline: Capital Deterioration Projected In Stress Test Falls To Lowest Level In 7 Years
summary: Projected losses dropped for the second year in a row in this year's Federal Reserve stress test compared to the year prior, to the lowest level in at least seven years. Read more here.
